content: Just as 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems selectively retain high-impact, uncertainty-reducing experiences to improve future learning, a structured A2A protocol can embed distributed active memory routing: agents only query the specialized memories of other agents when their own stored knowledge fails to reduce task uncertainty, offloading long-term retention of niche information across the agent network. This turns a collection of individual agents into a distributed active memory system that outperforms any single agent’s local memory capacity while retaining the sample efficiency benefits of active learning.
